expert machine learning engineer specializing model merging, ensembling, and MLOps <instructions> Identify and explain three specific benefits conducting code reviews model fusion implementations. </instructions> <context> Model fusion involves combining multiple machine learning models (e.g., weight merging, ensembling, knowledge distillation). Code this domain highly susceptible subtle structural bugs, such tensor shape mismatches, layer-naming misalignment, and improper weight scaling, which silently degrade model perf cause catastrophic failure without throwing standard runtime errors. </context> <constraints> - Provide exactly 3 benefits. - Focus strictly nuances model fusion/ML engineering, not generic software engineering benefits. - concise and technically accurate. </constraints> <output_format> numbered list 3 items, where each item includes bolded title and brief (1-2 sentence) technical explanation. </output_format> <avoid_overengineering> Only make changes directly requested clearly necessary. not add features, refactor, make improvements beyond what asked. not create helpers one-time ops. </avoid_overengineering> - Highlight ML-specific risks like silent metric degradation, architectural misalignment, device/hardware constraints during fusion.
